questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The company is committed ____ improving safety.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The Industrial Revolution transformed manufacturing processes. Q: It began in England in the late 18th century. R: Steam engines powered factories and railways. S: This led to unprecedented economic growth.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: ____ the rain, the match was cancelled.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Inflation reduces the purchasing power of money. Q: Central banks adjust interest rates to control inflation. R: High inflation can erode savings and reduce living standards. S: Moderate inflation is considered healthy for economic growth.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The government appealed ____ the public to stay calm.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Biodiversity is essential for ecosystem health. Q: Each species plays a unique role in its habitat. R: Loss of biodiversity threatens ecosystem stability. S: Conservation efforts are crucial to protect endangered species.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: ____ the circumstances, we decided to postpone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The Green Revolution increased agricultural productivity. Q: High-yield crop varieties were developed and introduced. R: Chemical fertilisers and pesticides were used extensively. S: This helped many countries achieve food security.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The committee consists ____ ten members.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Water scarcity is affecting many regions of India. Q: Groundwater levels are depleting at alarming rates. R: Efficient irrigation techniques can help conserve water. S: Rainwater harvesting is also being promoted.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He has been suffering ____ fever for a week.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: It is the second largest producer of coal in the world. Q: India has vast mineral resources. R: It also has significant reserves of iron ore and bauxite. S: The mining sector contributes substantially to the economy.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: No sooner did he arrive ____ the meeting began.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Urbanisation is increasing rapidly worldwide. Q: People migrate to cities in search of better opportunities. R: This puts pressure on housing and infrastructure. S: Sustainable urban planning is needed to manage growth.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The book is superior ____ the other one.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Coral reefs are among the most diverse ecosystems. Q: They provide habitat for numerous marine species. R: Climate change and ocean acidification threaten their survival. S: Conservation programmes are working to protect and restore reefs.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The teacher was satisfied ____ the results.

questions.stem: Find the error: She not only (A) / lost her wallet (B) / but also (C) / her mobile phone. (D)
options: A - should be 'She lost not only'
questions.solution: A - should be 'She lost not only'

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Gantt chart shows:
questions.solution: Gantt: bar chart of activities vs time.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Animation in simulation provides:
questions.solution: Animation: visual display of model running. Helps validate model, communicate results, identify bottlenecks.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 4, tm = 5, tp = 12 days. Its expected time is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Goniometer is used to measure:
questions.solution: Goniometer: measures joint angles. Essential for ergonomic assessment, rehab, work-station design. Universal or digital types.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Exponential smoothing family (ETS):
options: Error (A, M), Trend (N, A, Ad), Season (N, A, M). AICc minimization for model selection
questions.solution: ETS: state space models. A = additive, M = multiplicative, N = none, Ad = damped trend. Hyndman.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MRP (Material Requirements Planning) explodes the master schedule using the:
options: Bill of materials and inventory records
questions.solution: MRP nets requirements from the MPS, BOM and on-hand inventory to plan orders.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Critical Chain Project Management (CCPM) uses:
questions.solution: CCPM: Goldratt. Resources constrained. Feeding buffers and project buffer. Student syndrome. Merging with TOC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bullwhip quantification — BQ:
options: BQ = σ²(order) / σ²(demand). BQ > 1 = amplification. Beer game (MIT)
questions.solution: Bullwhip: Forrester effect. Minimum BQ = 1. Mitigation: VMI, POS sharing, EDI.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Strategic alignment: horizontally integrates:
questions.solution: Horizontal: consistency across functions. Operations supports marketing strategy, finance supports both. Must align.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Duality: primal max problem, dual min problem. Weak duality theorem:
options: Dual objective value >= Primal objective value (for any feasible solutions)
questions.solution: Weak duality: dual solution upper bounds primal. Strong duality: equal at optimality.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Cross-docking: goods are:
options: Transferred directly from inbound to outbound (minimal storage)
questions.solution: Cross-dock: receive and ship within 24h. Minimal handling/storage. Requires accurate timing and information.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bootstrap SPC:
options: Nonparametric control limits using bootstrap samples. For non-normal data. No distribution assumption
questions.solution: Bootstrap: resample from data. Replacements. B = 1000 samples. Limits from percentiles (25th/975th).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Product layout:
options: Sequential arrangement (assembly line). Low cost per unit, high output. Low flexibility
questions.solution: Product: balanced line. Cycle time = bottleneck. Efficiency = sum of task times / (CT × stations).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Design of Experiments: resolution IV design can estimate:
options: Main effects and two-factor interactions (some confounded)
questions.solution: Resolution IV: no main effects confounded with 2FI. Some 2FI confounded with each other. Practical for screening.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An activity's normal time/cost are 8 days/₹3000 and crash time/cost are 5 days/₹5000. Its crash cost slope is:
questions.solution: Cost slope = (crash − normal cost)/(normal − crash time) = (5000−3000)/(8−5) = ₹666.7/day.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Snook and Ciriello tables provide:
questions.solution: S& C tables: psychophysical data for manual tasks. Percentile values (usually 75th or 90th). Based on population studies.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Periodic review (R,S) policy: review period R=7 days, lead time L=3 days, daily demand=50:
options: Order up to S = 50*(7+3) + safety stock
questions.solution: Order up to: S = demand during (R+L) + SS. Protects for R+L period.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Component A (R = 0.95) is in series with a parallel pair B (0.8) and C (0.9). The system reliability is about:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: FAST diagram shows:
questions.solution: FAST: HOW-WHY logic. Left to right: higher to lower order.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: 5S methodology: Seiri means:
questions.solution: 5S: Sort (Seiri), Set in order (Seiton), Shine (Seiso), Standardize (Seiketsu), Sustain (Shitsuke). Japanese workplace organization.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Concurrent engineering reduces:
questions.solution: Concurrent: parallel activities (design + process planning). Communication between teams. Reduces iteration and delays.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹50000, price = ₹80/unit, variable cost = ₹60/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Real options in project valuation provide:
options: Value of flexibility to make future decisions
questions.solution: Real options: value of managerial flexibility (expand, abandon, delay). NPV + option value = total project value.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aggregate planning: level strategy:
options: Constant production rate. Use inventory and backorders to absorb demand variation
questions.solution: Level: stable workforce. Build inventory in low demand. Use in high demand.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A task has a normal time of 8 min with 15% allowances. Its standard time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Process capability: Cpk = min[(USL-μ)/(3σ), (μ-LSL)/(3σ)]. For μ=10, σ=0.5, USL=12, LSL=8:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: GOLF (Government-Open-Local-Foreign):
options: By source. Government (regulatory), Open (general), Local, Foreign
questions.solution: GOLF: procurement classification. Government: specific regulations. Foreign: import procedures.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Component A (R = 0.95) is in series with a parallel pair B (0.8) and C (0.95). The system reliability is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aggregate planning sets production levels over a horizon of about:
options: Several months to a year (medium term)
questions.solution: Aggregate (medium-term) planning balances capacity and demand over months.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Cycle time definition:
options: Time between successive units off line
questions.solution: Cycle time: time between units. Inversely related to production rate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NPV: Initial cost=$20000, annual benefit=$6000, r=8%, n=5 years. NPV≈

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Third-party logistics (3PL):
options: Outsourced logistics services (warehousing, transportation, freight forwarding)
questions.solution: 3PL: DHL, FedEx, UPS, XPO. 4PL: lead logistics provider, manages multiple 3PLs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Preventive maintenance: scheduled maintenance at fixed intervals:
options: Time-based. Reduces failures but may be unnecessary (over-maintenance)
questions.solution: PM: calendar or usage-based. Trade-off: PM cost vs failure cost. Too frequent = waste.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Analytical estimation:
options: Estimating time by breaking job into elements from past experience/knowledge
questions.solution: Analytical: for non-repetitive/maintenance jobs. Less accurate. Speed and rest allowances.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A worker's observed time is 6 min at a performance rating of 80%. The normal time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A worker's observed time is 5 min at a performance rating of 80%. The normal time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A worker's observed time is 5 min at a performance rating of 100%. The normal time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PMTS (Methods-Time Measurement): 1 TMU = 0.036 seconds
questions.solution: TMU: time measurement unit. 1 TMU = 0.036 sec = 0.00001 hr. 100,000 TMU = 1 hr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability of parallel system: R_p = 1 - (1-R₁)(1-R₂)...(1-R_n). For 2 components R=0.9:
questions.solution: Parallel: at least one must work. Much more reliable than series. Redundancy increases reliability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Finite capacity scheduling (FCS):
options: Constraint-based scheduling. Gantt chart with capacity constraints. Forward or backward scheduling
questions.solution: FCS: software (Preactor, Asprova). Rule: SPT for flow time, EDD for due date. Bottleneck focus.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The reorder point (under constant demand) is given by:
options: Demand rate × lead time (+ safety stock)
questions.solution: Reorder point = demand during lead time, plus safety stock if demand/lead time vary.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 4 and tp = 16 days. Its standard deviation is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: EOQ: D=10000/yr, S=$50, H=$5/unit/yr. Q*=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CPM — Critical Path Method:
questions.solution: CPM: activity-on-node (AON). Critical path: longest path. Float: LS-ES or LF-EF.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Method study procedure:
options: Select → Record → Examine → Develop → Install → Maintain (SREDIM)
questions.solution: SREDIM: Select job, Record facts, Examine critically, Develop method, Install, Maintain.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NPV: Initial=$15000, annual=$4000, r=12%, n=6yr. NPV=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Taguchi loss function: L(x) = k(x-T)². For k=100, x=10.5, T=10:
questions.solution: Taguchi: any deviation from target incurs cost. Quadratic loss. k = cost of deviation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Total Productive Maintenance (TPM):
options: Zero breakdowns, zero defects, zero accidents. Autonomous maintenance by operators
questions.solution: TPM: 5 pillars. OEE = Availability × Performance × Quality. World-class OEE > 85%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Plant layout types:
questions.solution: Product: high volume, standardized. Process: job shop, flexible. Cellular: GT families.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Holt-Winters triple exponential smoothing includes:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cross-docking:
options: Shipments sorted and transferred directly without storage. Reduced inventory
questions.solution: Cross-dock: Wal-Mart model. Receiving → sorting → loading. Less-than-truckload consolidation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Daily demand = 40, lead time = 5 days, safety stock = 100 units. The reorder point is:
questions.solution: ROP = (demand × lead time) + safety stock = 40×5 + 100 = 300 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An activity's normal time/cost are 8 days/₹3000 and crash time/cost are 4 days/₹8000. Its crash cost slope is:
questions.solution: Cost slope = (crash − normal cost)/(normal − crash time) = (8000−3000)/(8−4) = ₹1250/day.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹20000, price = ₹80/unit, variable cost = ₹50/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 4 and tp = 11 days. Its standard deviation is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost ₹30000, price ₹100, variable cost ₹70/unit. The output needed for a target profit of ₹20000 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 5+6, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 2+5), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 11, 1-3-4 = 7. The critical path is the longest = 11 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about the break-even point is correct?
options: Break-even is where variable cost is zero
Below it the firm makes a loss; above it a profit
questions.solution: Break-even is zero profit; output above it yields profit, below it a loss.

questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹50000, price = ₹100/unit, variable cost = ₹50/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PERT differs from CPM mainly in that PERT treats activity times as:
questions.solution: PERT uses optimistic, most-likely and pessimistic estimates; CPM uses single deterministic times.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Queuing — G/G/1:
options: General arrival and service. No closed form. Kingman's approximation: L ≈ ρ + (ρ²·(c_a² + c_s²))/(2·(1-ρ))
questions.solution: Kingman: c_a, c_s = coefficients of variation. Heavy traffic (ρ→1) approximation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cold chain logistics:
options: Temperature-controlled supply chain. Cold storage (refrigerated warehouse) + reefer trucks. Pharma (vaccine), food
questions.solution: Cold chain: HACCP, GDP. 2-8°C (pharma), -18°C (frozen). Active vs passive packaging.

questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 10, 1-3-4 = 11. The critical path is the longest = 11 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Taguchi loss function: quality loss increases quadratically with deviation from target.
options: Smaller deviation is always better (loss not zero until exactly on target)
questions.solution: Taguchi: any deviation from nominal has cost. L(x) = k(x-T)^2. Target = zero loss.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DOE — fractional factorial:
options: 2^(k-p) designs. Fewer runs. Aliasing (confounding) of effects. Resolution III, IV, V
questions.solution: Resolution III: main effects confounded with 2-way. V: main effects and 2-way clear. Design generators.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A worker's observed time is 5 min at a performance rating of 110%. The normal time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Weibull distribution:
options: β < 1: early life. β = 1: random (exponential). β > 1: wear-out. Shape parameter
questions.solution: Weibull: reliability R(t) = exp(-(t/η)^β). η = characteristic life. β = shape, η = scale.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hungarian method steps:
options: Row reduction, column reduction, line covering, augmentation
questions.solution: Hungarian: O(n³). Minimization. For maximization: negate costs or subtract from max.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PERT probability: project completion within 20 days if mean=18, std=2. Z=(20-18)/2=1:
questions.solution: Z=1: from standard normal table, P(Z<1) = 0.8413 = 84.1%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Assignment — prohibited:
options: Very high cost M. Certain assignments not allowed
questions.solution: Prohibited: M > max cost. If forced assignment to M → infeasible.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Simplex tableau: entering variable selected by most negative (z_j - c_j) for:
questions.solution: Max: most negative reduced cost enters. Min: most positive. Bland's rule: smallest index to avoid cycling.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vogel's Approximation Method (VAM):
options: Heuristic for initial solution. Based on penalty (difference between two smallest costs)
questions.solution: VAM: row/column penalties. Allocate to smallest cost in max-penalty row/column. Near-optimal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Assignment problem:
options: Assign n jobs to n machines (1:1). Hungarian method. Minimize cost/time
questions.solution: Hungarian: subtract row/col minima, cover zeros, iterate. Optimal: n assignments.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A process has USL = 110, LSL = 90, mean = 102, σ = 3. Its Cpk is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: OHSAS 18001 / ISO 45001:
options: Occupational health and safety management. Risk-based approach
questions.solution: ISO 45001: hazard identification, risk assessment, control hierarchy (elimination > PPE).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Control chart — R chart:
options: Monitors process variability (range within samples). Centerline = average range
questions.solution: R chart: UCL = D₄·R̄, LCL = D₃·R̄. Before X-bar: check R-chart for stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kaizen:
options: Continuous incremental improvement. Small changes, everyone involved
questions.solution: Kaizen: 5S (Sort, Set, Shine, Standardize, Sustain). Poka-yoke (mistake proofing).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Effective capacity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: (Q,R) policy — continuous review:
options: Order Q when inventory hits R. R = μ_L + z·σ_L. Optimization: min K·D/Q + h(Q/2 + R - μ_L)
questions.solution: (Q,R): Q from EOQ. R from service level. Fill rate vs cycle service level. Approximate: Hadley-Whitin.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A task has a normal time of 5 min with 15% allowances. Its standard time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Global supply chain — total landed cost:
options: Purchase price + freight + duty + insurance + inventory carrying + risk. Nearshoring calculation
questions.solution: TLC: used for sourcing decisions. Soft factors: IP risk, geopolitical stability.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Bottleneck shift after exploiting constraint:
questions.solution: After elevating one constraint, another may become bottleneck. Repeat entire cycle. Continuous improvement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Corrective maintenance: maintenance performed after failure occurs.
questions.solution: Corrective: fix when broken. Lower planned cost, higher unplanned cost (downtime, secondary damage).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: CPM assumes:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FMECA (Failure Mode Effects & Criticality Analysis):
questions.solution: FMECA: criticality matrix. Failure mode probability level (A to E). Severity class (I to IV).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Variance reduction: antithetic variates technique uses:
questions.solution: Antithetic: if U is random, use 1-U as companion. Negative correlation reduces variance of mean estimate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Daily demand = 60, lead time = 5 days, safety stock = 80 units. The reorder point is:
questions.solution: ROP = (demand × lead time) + safety stock = 60×5 + 80 = 380 units.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: JIT (Just-in-Time) goals include:
options: Zero waste, zero defects, zero inventory
questions.solution: JIT: produce what is needed, when needed, in needed quantity. Eliminate waste. Requires stable demand, quality, suppliers.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Verification in simulation means:
questions.solution: Verification: debugging. Model does what intended. Validation: model represents reality.两者 distinct but complementary.
